ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Nɔkorɛ meeka akyerɛ ho, ɔbɛma saa akoa he adi n’agyapadeɛ nyinaa so.
translated: If someone does not tell him, he will make the servant carry all his loads.

ref: Respect your father and mother. And love others as much as you love yourself.”
text: Di w’agya ne wo na nni; na afei, Dɔ wo yɔnko sɛ wo ho.”
translated: Don't be angry with him; and now, love him as yourself.”

ref: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly surprised and asked, “How can anyone ever be saved?”
text: Asuafoɔ he tee yei he, ɔyɛɛ bɛ nwanwa kɛse, na bɛkaa sɛ “Sei ne ɔteɛ deɛ a, hwane ne betumi anya nkwa?”
translated: The chiefs gathered and said to him, "If you are a wise man, who can you give the opportunity to?"

ref: he took the seven loaves of bread and the fish and gave thanks. He then broke them and handed them to his disciples, who passed them around to the crowds.
text: na afei ɔfaa paanoo nson ne nkyenam he; na ɔdaa ase wieeɛ he, obubuu mu mmaa asuafoɔ he, na asuafoɔ he nso de maa nnipadɔm he.
translated: He took a handful of the flour and threw it into the sea; he also poured some into the pot, and the pot boiled over, and the people were fed.

ref: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Na mmeema anifirafoɔ mmienu bi tete kwane nkyɛne. Berɛ a bɛtee sɛ Yesu ootwam he, bɛteaam sɛ, “Awurade hu yɛ mmɔbɔ, Dawid Ba!”
translated: And lo, two blind men sitting by the roadside, having heard that Jesus was passing by, cried out, saying, “Lord, have mercy on us, Son of David!”

ref: No one uses a new piece of cloth to patch old clothes. The patch would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: Biaa mfa ntoma mono sini ntɛ ataadeɛ dada mu, anyɛ saa a, ntɛmuu he bɛte afiri mu ama ne nteɛ he ayɛ kɛse paa.
translated: If you don't listen to your mother, she will say that you are stubborn and that is why you are suffering.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Na onim bɛ adwene, nti ɔka kyerɛɛ bɛ sɛ, “Ahennie biaa a ɔne ne ho di asie he bɛsɛe, na kuro anaa fie biaa a ɔko tia ne ho he, onnyina.
translated: When they were thinking, he said to them, “Every kingdom that is divided against itself will be laid waste, and every city or house that is divided against itself will not stand.

ref: The man talked about it so much and told so many people, that Jesus could no longer go openly into a town. He had to stay away from the towns, but people still came to him from everywhere.
text: Nso ofirii adi, na ɔhyɛɛ aseɛ kekaa ho asɛm, ma ɔtrɛɛ maa afei deɛ Yesu antumi anyi n’anim ankɔ kuro biaa mu bio, na ɔtenaa nkuro agya, na nnipa firii mmeammea baa ne nkyɛne.
translated: He therefore left that place and went into the region of Judea, and there again he crossed over to Jerusalem; and many people came to him; and he was teaching them.

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: Wamma biaa anni n’akyi gye Petro, Yakubu ne Yohane, Yakubu nua he.
translated: Peter, James and John, James got it.

ref: But the guests did not pay any attention. Some of them left for their farms, and some went to their places of business.
text: Nso bɛamfa n’asɛm he annyɛ hwee na bɛkɔɔ bɛ baabi, baako kɔɔ n’afuom, bi nso kɔɔ n’adwuma,
translated: He will not listen to anyone's words and will do as he pleases, one will go to his farm, and another will go to his work,

ref: Anyone who swears by the altar also swears by everything on it.
text: Nti biaa a ɔde afɔrebokyia ka ntam he ka ɔne deɛ ɔya so nyinaa ntam;
translated: He does not take a bribe and does not use his position to oppress anyone.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet had said,
text: Yei nyinaa siiɛ maa deɛ diyifoɔ he kaeɛ he baam, sɛ,
translated: We are going to eat before you get angry

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: na nnipadɔm he sane behyiaa mu a na bennya aduane mpo nni.
translated: If you do not listen to your elders, you will not get good advice.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: W’ahennie mmra. Wopɛ nyɛ hɔ, asaase yi so sɛdeɛ ɔteɛ soro.
translated: The world is in a bad state. You want to be somewhere else, on this earth, as if you are in heaven.

ref: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
text: Befirii adi ɔɔkɔ he, bɛtoo Kireneni beema bi a bɛfrɛ no Simon; ɔno ne bɛhyɛɛ no maa no soaa Yesu asennua he.
translated: They called the name of the place where he was born, Kireneni, and they named him Simon; he was the one they say was born with the power of Jesus.

ref: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
text: Ɔnsɛ sɛ nka wohu wo yɔnko akoa mmɔbɔ, sɛdeɛ mehuu wo mmɔbɔ he anaa?’
translated: Don't you see that I have seen your nakedness, how I saw you naked?

ref: They sat down in groups of
text: Na bɛtenaa akuo akuo, ha ha ne aduonum aduonum.
translated: They are all the same, there is no difference

ref: He told them, “Go to Bethlehem and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, let me know. I also want to go and worship him.”
text: Na ɔsomaa bɛ Yerusalem, kaa sɛ, “Honkɔhwehwɛ abɔfra he yie; na sɛ hohu no a, hommɛka nkyerɛ me na me nso menkɔkoto no bi.”
translated: I have sent my servant to Jerusalem, saying, “Go and find out about this child; and when you see him, inform me so that I may also go and worship him.”

ref: At once the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. The earth shook, and rocks split apart.
text: Saa berɛ he, ntoma a otwa asɔredane he mu he tee mmienu firi soro de kosii fam, na asaase wosoeɛ, na abotane paepaeɛ.
translated: The sky and the earth were separated, and the earth was formed, and the trees sprouted.

ref: Everyone was amazed at his teaching. He taught with authority, and not like the teachers of the Law of Moses.
text: Ne nkyerɛkyerɛ he yɛɛ bɛ nwanwa, esiane sɛ ɔde tumi paa ne kasaeɛ, na wankasa sɛ atwerɛfoɔ he.
translated: He does not joke with anyone, because he has great strength and a fierce tongue, and people do not joke with him.
